titleStmt.title: SII 20.143: original edition by G. S. Gai
No. 143. (B.K. No. 63 of 1936-37.) KOṆḌGUḶI, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.hi: Broken pillar set up on the bund of the well outside the village.

summary.p: This damaged record is dated in the king's regnal year 11, Vyaya, Śrāvaṇa, śu. 14, Thursday. The tithi given corresponds to A.D. 1166, July 13, which was a Wednesday.
The inscription seems to register a gift made to the mahājanas of agrahāra Koṇḍaguḷi by Nakharas and others. Mentions Kumāra Mallugi. The king is stated to be ruling from his capital [Kaṅgāra].

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
© svasti śrīmatu bhuvanāśraya pri
thvīvallabha mahārājādhirāja para
mēśvara paramabhaṭṭāraka kaḷacuriya ku
ḷatiḷaka sakaḷaḷōkābharaṇa śrīma
dbhujabaḻacakravarttiyara vijayarājyamu
ttarōttarābhivṛddhipravarddhamānamācaṁdrā
rkkatāraṁbaraṁ saluttamire kaṁgārada
nelevīḍinalu sukhasaṁkathāvinōdadiṁ rājyaṁ
geyyuttamirddu bhujabaḷa cakrava
rttiyara 11 neya byaya saṁvatsarada
srāvaṇa suddha caturddasi brahaspativā
parbbadaṁdu śrīmatu
namasyadagrahāra koṁḍaguḷiya
mahājanaṁgaḷige pādapū
śrīmatu kumāra malugi
maneverggaḍe mahāpradhāna
nāyakara

ru nakharaṁgaḷu
ge mūlevata saṭṭuga Ā
ḷa soṭṭige yeṇneya koṭṭaru
I dharmavaṁ Ārasugaḷuṁ mahā
janaṁgaḷuṁ mahēśvararu taḷada ba
ḷaṁjigaruṁ nakharaṁgaḷuṁ śāsanamā
rggadalu naḍesalavarige puṇyada pha
ḷavāvudeṁdaḍe prithiviya mēga
ṇa śrīgaṁgege modalāgi divya tīrtthaga
ḷalu divyakṣētraṁgaḷalu divya dē
vatāsthānaṁgaḷalella nityānīka sahaRead nityānēka.
sraliṁgavaṁ pratiṣṭheyaṁ māḍi brā
hmaṇarige bhūmidāna suvarṇnadānavaṁ
koṭṭa phala I dharmmavaṁ kiḍisida pāpi
gaḷu ghōravappa narakaṁgaḷalu bīḷvarū © ©
sāmānyōyaṁ dharmmasētu nripāṇāṁ
kālē kālē pālanīyō bhavadbhiḥ sarvvānētān
bhāvinaḥ pārtthivēṁdrān bhūyō bhūyō yā
catē rāmabhadra � svadattaṁ paradattaṁ vā yō
harētu vasuṁdharāṁ ṣaṣṭhirvvarṣasaha
srāṇi viṣṭāyāṁ jāyatē krimi .
Digital edition of SII 20.143 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
